title: Peach Farm is closing — but only temporarily
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Peach Farm in Boston's Chinatown is closing temporarily as current owners retire and new management takes over.
Current owner Tom Leung confirmed retirement after 31 years; new ownership by former East Ocean Seafood operators.
The Boston Licensing Board approved the transfer of Peach Farm’s licenses, maintaining its name and concept.

title: Mass. U.S. attorney launches benefit, voter fraud task force
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts U.S. Attorney Leah Foley formed a task force to investigate benefit and voter fraud statewide.
The Benefit and Voter Fraud Team will focus on SNAP, MassHealth, childcare subsidies, and election-related offenses.
A public hotline, 1-855-SCAM-MA-1, was launched to report suspected fraud in Massachusetts.

title: United to offer couch-style economy seating — would you try it?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces Relax Row, a couch-style seating option available in economy class starting in 2027.
Relax Row features three seats transforming into a flat space, with mattress pad, blankets, and pillows provided.
Relax Row will be offered in over 200 Boeing 787 and 777 planes by 2030, with up to 12 sections per aircraft.

title: Ad Club’s Women’s Leadership Forum stays alive amid DEI pushback
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Ad Club's annual Women's Leadership Forum was held at Gillette Stadium with over 1,000 attendees.
The event included speakers, panel discussions, and a marketplace, focusing on empowering women in business amidst DEI reassessment.
Featured speakers like Nobel Laureate Nadia Murad emphasized resilience and the importance of personal and professional connections.

title: Juvenile injured in Allston shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police investigate a shooting injuring a juvenile near 222 Brighton Ave in Allston on Thursday evening.
The victim, found at 10 Allston St, was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries, according to police.
The shooter fled the scene and no arrests have been made as of the latest police update.

title: He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Steven Iannacone is running his 11th Boston Marathon to support Boston Children's Hospital and the Miles for Miracles program.
Iannacone has been running for Jace Perkins for a decade, starting after a chance meeting with Jace's father.
Jace Perkins, once a premature NICU baby, is now a thriving and healthy 12-year-old thanks to Boston Children's Hospital.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston news stories.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor, has held the position since 2019.
The quiz covers various trending topics and events happening in the Greater Boston area.

title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox utilized MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System effectively in a 3-0 Opening Day win over the Reds.
A successful ninth inning challenge by Roman Anthony overturned a strike call, leading to a crucial walk.
Anthony's challenge was pivotal in extending the inning, enabling additional runs and securing a win for Boston.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer made his Red Sox Opening Day debut as a pinch-hitter, going 2-for-2 and scoring two runs in a 3-0 win.
Drafted 4th overall in 2021, Mayer wasn't guaranteed a starting spot due to last year's offensive struggles and injury issues.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora praised Mayer's pinch-hit impact, which helped break a 0-0 tie against the Cincinnati Reds.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog was saved by Campton-Thornton firefighters after cutting his leg on a solar light.
Firefighters applied a tourniquet to stop Ziggy's severe bleeding before he underwent surgery.
A veterinarian confirmed the quick actions by firefighters saved Ziggy's life from excessive blood loss.
